items.q: How often should I service my AC in South Florida?
items.a: We recommend at least twice a year — once before summer and once before winter. South Florida's heat and humidity make your AC work harder than in most places, so regular maintenance prevents breakdowns and keeps your energy bills low.

items.q: How long does a typical AC installation take?
items.a: Most residential installations are completed in one day (4-8 hours). Commercial installations may take longer depending on the size of the system. We'll give you an accurate timeline during your free estimate.

items.q: Do you offer financing for new AC systems?
items.a: Yes! We understand that a new AC system is a significant investment. We offer flexible financing options to help you get the comfort you need without breaking the bank. Call us for details.

items.q: What brands do you work with?
items.a: We service and install all major brands including Carrier, Trane, Lennox, Rheem, Goodman, Daikin, and more. We'll help you choose the best brand and model for your needs and budget.

items.q: Do you provide service in both English and Spanish?
items.a: Absolutely! Our team is fully bilingual. We have dedicated English and Spanish phone lines to make sure you can communicate comfortably in your preferred language.

items.q: What should I do if my AC stops working in the middle of the night?
items.a: Call us! We offer 24/7 emergency repair service. Our technicians are available day and night, weekends and holidays. There are no after-hours surprise fees.

items.q: How do I know if I need a repair or a full replacement?
items.a: Generally, if your system is over 15 years old and needs frequent repairs, replacement is often more cost-effective. If it's newer and the repair is straightforward, we'll fix it. We always give you honest advice — we'll never push a replacement you don't need.
We serve all of South Florida including Miami, Hialeah, Homestead, Coral Gables, Doral, Kendall, and surrounding areas in Miami-Dade County.

miami.name: Miami
miami.desc: Comprehensive AC services for Miami's diverse neighborhoods — from Downtown and Brickell to Little Havana and Wynwood. We keep Miami cool year-round.
miami.longDesc: As the heart of South Florida, Miami demands reliable air conditioning. With average summer temperatures in the 90s and humidity that can make it feel even hotter, your AC system works overtime. MGM A/C Appliances has been serving Miami homeowners and businesses with expert installation, repair, and maintenance services. Whether you're in a high-rise condo in Brickell, a family home in Westchester, or a restaurant in Little Havana — we've got you covered.
miami.housingInfo: Miami housing is a mix of pre-1960s masonry homes in neighborhoods like Flagami and Westchester, modern glass-and-steel high-rise condos in Brickell and Downtown, and everything in between. Older homes often have oversized duct systems that leak significantly, while high-rises rely on chilled-water systems or individual fan coil units. A large portion of the single-family stock was built without central AC in mind, meaning ductwork was retrofitted through closets and attic spaces.
miami.climateChallenge: Miami sits in a humid subtropical climate with average summer heat indices exceeding 100°F, putting extraordinary strain on AC equipment that runs virtually nonstop from April through October. Traffic pollution — especially near I-95 and SR-836 — loads air filters faster than in any other South Florida city, sometimes requiring monthly filter changes instead of quarterly. The urban heat island effect means Miami nights rarely cool below 80°F, leaving systems with no overnight recovery period.
commonIssues: Clogged air filters from urban pollution and construction dust — check monthly
Refrigerant leaks in aging mini-splits and older split systems in pre-1980 homes
Drain line blockages caused by constant high-humidity operation and algae growth
Fan coil unit corrosion in high-rise condos due to concentrated humidity and limited ventilation
miami.localTip: Miami homes running AC 24/7 should schedule bi-annual maintenance — once before summer in April and once after hurricane season in November — to catch refrigerant issues, clean coils, and flush drain lines before small problems become expensive breakdowns.

testimonial.text: Our Brickell condo AC unit stopped cooling in the middle of July. MGM had a tech out within two hours and had us up and running the same day. Incredible service in the Miami heat!
testimonial.name: Carlos M.

hialeah.name: Hialeah
hialeah.desc: Trusted AC repair and installation in Hialeah and Hialeah Gardens. Fast, bilingual service your family can count on.
hialeah.longDesc: Hialeah is one of the most densely populated cities in Florida, and when the AC goes out, you need fast service. MGM A/C Appliances provides same-day repair service throughout Hialeah and Hialeah Gardens. Our bilingual team is ready to help — most of our Hialeah customers prefer Spanish, and we're happy to serve you in your preferred language.
hialeah.housingInfo: Hialeah is dominated by concrete block homes built in the 1960s through the 1980s, most with original central air systems that have been repaired and patched over the decades rather than replaced. Many residences run older R-22 refrigerant systems that are increasingly difficult and expensive to service now that R-22 is phased out. Attic spaces in these flat-roofed and low-pitched homes trap enormous heat loads, forcing AC systems to fight temperatures above 130°F in the plenum.
hialeah.climateChallenge: Hialeah's inland location means no ocean breeze to moderate temperatures, and the city's dense urban fabric — with minimal tree canopy — creates a severe heat island effect. Homes built in the 1960s and 70s often lack proper insulation, meaning the AC system must compensate for poor building envelope performance. Aging electrical panels in older homes struggle to handle modern high-efficiency units, sometimes requiring panel upgrades before new AC installations.
commonIssues: R-22 refrigerant systems that are costly to repair and urgently need replacement with R-410A units
Undersized or improperly matched duct systems in 1960s-1980s homes causing hot and cold spots
Overheating compressors due to poor attic ventilation in flat-roofed concrete block homes
Electrical panel capacity issues preventing upgrade to higher-efficiency systems
hialeah.localTip: If your Hialeah home still has an R-22 system, plan your replacement now — R-22 refrigerant prices have quintupled since the phase-out and a single recharge can cost more than a new system's first year of energy savings. We offer bilingual consultations to help you choose the right replacement.

testimonial.text: MGM me ayudó a reemplazar mi viejo sistema de R-22 con uno nuevo y eficiente. Me explicaron todo en español y el precio fue muy justo. El A/C nuevo enfría mucho mejor y mi factura de electricidad bajó bastante.
testimonial.name: Maria G.

homestead.name: Homestead
homestead.desc: Reliable AC service in Homestead and Florida City. We understand the unique needs of South Dade homes.
homestead.longDesc: Homestead and Florida City sit at the southern tip of Miami-Dade County, where the heat can be relentless. Many homes in this area are newer construction, but the demanding South Florida climate means your AC still needs regular attention. MGM A/C Appliances serves the entire South Dade area with professional AC installation, repair, and maintenance.
homestead.housingInfo: Homestead and the surrounding agricultural communities represent one of South Florida's most economically diverse markets, with housing ranging from modest concrete block homes built in the 1950s-1970s to newer developments catering to first-time homebuyers. The area was ground zero for Hurricane Andrew in 1992, and much of the pre-Andrew housing stock was either destroyed and rebuilt or significantly damaged — meaning many homes were reconstructed under stricter post-Andrew building codes with better structural integrity but vary widely in insulation quality.
homestead.climateChallenge: Homestead sits further south than any other major South Florida municipality, meaning longer, hotter summers with temperatures that regularly reach 95-100°F and heat indices exceeding 110°F in July and August. The agricultural surroundings introduce unique air quality challenges — crop dusting chemicals, farm dust, and pollen from the farmlands can saturate filters in days during growing season. The area's distance from service centers and budget-conscious customer base means issues that might be caught early in wealthier areas often go unaddressed until systems fail completely.
commonIssues: Rapid air filter saturation from agricultural dust and pollen during growing and harvest seasons
Compressor failures from extreme heat loading — outdoor temperatures above 95°F dramatically reduce efficiency
Post-hurricane ductwork damage that was never properly inspected or repaired after major storms
Refrigerant leaks in aging systems running beyond their design life due to deferred replacement
homestead.localTip: Homestead homeowners near agricultural areas should use MERV-8 or higher filters and check them every 2-3 weeks during harvest season — farm dust and organic particles can completely block a standard filter in weeks, causing the system to freeze up and fail right when you need it most.

testimonial.text: My AC went out on the hottest day of the year and MGM came out to Homestead same day. They had parts on the truck and fixed it in under two hours. Very fair pricing too — I appreciated that they didn't try to oversell me.
testimonial.name: James T.

coralGables.name: Coral Gables
coralGables.desc: Premium AC services for Coral Gables' historic homes and modern residences. Licensed and trusted.
coralGables.longDesc: Coral Gables is known for its beautiful historic homes and tree-lined streets. Many of these older homes have unique HVAC challenges — from ductwork in tight spaces to systems that need to work with the home's architectural character. MGM A/C Appliances has experience working with both historic and modern Coral Gables properties, ensuring your home stays comfortable without compromising its beauty.
coralGables.housingInfo: Coral Gables is home to some of Miami-Dade's most architecturally significant housing — Mediterranean Revival estates from the 1920s and 1930s designed by George Merrick, with thick coral rock walls, barrel tile roofs, and floor plans that were never designed with central air conditioning in mind. Retrofit duct installations in these homes often run through closets, under floors, or in added soffits to preserve historic ceilings. The City's strict Historic Preservation Board oversees renovations and can limit how HVAC equipment is installed or displayed.
coralGables.climateChallenge: Coral Gables' beautiful mature tree canopy — one of Miami's densest — shades homes and reduces cooling loads, but falling debris and roots can damage outdoor condensers and drain lines. The coral rock construction that gives the city its name provides excellent thermal mass but makes routing new ductwork extremely challenging and expensive. Many historic homes use high-wall ductless mini-splits as the least invasive solution, though these must be tastefully concealed to satisfy HOA and preservation guidelines.
commonIssues: Ductwork routing challenges in historic coral rock homes requiring creative installation solutions
Tree debris blocking condenser coils and outdoor unit airflow in heavily landscaped properties
Historic Preservation Board permit requirements slowing equipment replacement approvals
Ductless mini-split placement restrictions imposed by HOA and historic preservation guidelines
coralGables.localTip: In Coral Gables historic homes, a ductless multi-zone mini-split system is often the best solution — it requires only a small hole through the wall for refrigerant lines, preserves your historic ceilings and trim, and qualifies for energy efficiency rebates while delivering far better cooling than window units.

testimonial.text: Our 1928 Mediterranean home had ancient window units in every room. MGM designed a beautiful multi-zone mini-split system that hides behind period-appropriate grilles. The Preservation Board approved it and we're finally comfortable in every room.
testimonial.name: Patricia H.

doral.name: Doral
doral.desc: Commercial and residential AC experts in Doral. Serving the city's growing business community.
doral.longDesc: Doral is one of South Florida's fastest-growing cities, with a thriving business community and expanding residential areas. MGM A/C Appliances serves both commercial and residential customers in Doral, from office parks and retail centers to single-family homes and apartments. Our commercial HVAC expertise makes us the right choice for Doral businesses.
doral.housingInfo: Doral is one of Miami-Dade's newest and fastest-growing cities, with housing stock primarily from the 2000s through the present — modern townhomes, gated communities, and mixed-use developments near the Doral Golf Resort. The Venezuelan, Colombian, and broader Latin American community that defines Doral tends toward well-maintained, newer construction. Many homes in Doral feature modern high-efficiency systems with variable-speed technology and smart thermostats, though the commercial corridor along NW 36th and 41st Streets has significant rooftop package unit inventory.
doral.climateChallenge: Doral's location near Miami International Airport and major freight logistics facilities means elevated particulate matter in the air, accelerating filter clogging and increasing the frequency of coil cleaning needed. The rapid residential construction boom has also produced some installations where equipment was sized for quick sale rather than optimal performance — oversized systems that short-cycle and fail to dehumidify properly. Doral's flat inland terrain concentrates heat with no ocean moderation.
commonIssues: Oversized systems in newer homes that short-cycle and leave humid air even when temperature setpoint is met
Commercial rooftop package unit failures on the busy NW 36th Street corridor
Air filter over-saturation from airport and freight corridor particulate matter
Smart thermostat connectivity issues in homes with complex home automation systems
doral.localTip: If your Doral home feels cool but still humid, your system may be oversized — an AC that's too large cools the air quickly but doesn't run long enough to remove moisture. We can perform a Manual J load calculation to confirm the right size and, if needed, install a whole-home dehumidifier to solve the comfort issue without replacing the entire system.

testimonial.text: Nuestro sistema nuevo se instaló muy rápido pero la casa siempre se sentía húmeda. MGM identificó que el equipo era demasiado grande para nuestra casa y nos instalaron un deshumidificador. Ahora el ambiente es perfecto.
testimonial.name: Alejandro V.

kendall.name: Kendall
kendall.desc: Your neighborhood AC company in Kendall. Fast response, honest pricing, bilingual service.
kendall.longDesc: Kendall is one of Miami-Dade's largest residential communities, and reliable AC is a must. With summer temperatures regularly hitting the 90s and humidity that can make it feel like 100+, you need an AC company you can trust. MGM A/C Appliances provides fast, affordable AC service throughout the Kendall area, from The Falls to Dadeland.
kendall.housingInfo: Kendall is sprawling suburban South Florida at its most expansive — large single-family homes on generous lots, many built from the 1970s through the 1990s, with floor plans exceeding 2,500 square feet that require substantial AC capacity. Duct runs in Kendall homes are among the longest in Miami-Dade, stretching from a centrally-located air handler to bedrooms at opposite ends of the home, creating pressure imbalances and temperature variation. The area's mix of ranch-style and two-story homes means both single-zone and multi-zone systems are common.
kendall.climateChallenge: The sheer size of Kendall homes means undersized systems are a frequent problem — a 3-ton unit that's sufficient for a 1,500 sq ft home in Hialeah may be dramatically insufficient for a 3,000 sq ft Kendall ranch. Long duct runs accumulate significant leakage over time, and many Kendall homes have ductwork that's never been pressure-tested or sealed since original installation. The distance from the coast means no salt air issues but also no sea breeze to moderate temperatures during the hottest months.
commonIssues: Temperature imbalance between rooms due to long duct runs and pressure distribution issues
Undersized equipment struggling to cool large floor plans during peak summer heat
Duct leakage in aging flex duct systems that have never been pressure-tested or sealed
Zoning system failures in two-story homes where upstairs always stays warmer than downstairs
kendall.localTip: Kendall homeowners with large floor plans should request an airflow balancing assessment along with any service call — we use digital manometers to measure static pressure at each register, which reveals exactly which rooms are being starved of airflow and whether your system is truly delivering its rated capacity throughout the house.

testimonial.text: Our master bedroom at the end of the house was always 5 degrees warmer than the living room. MGM discovered two disconnected duct sections in the attic and a restricted return grille. Now every room feels the same — what a difference!
testimonial.name: Robert K.

miamiBeach.name: Miami Beach
miamiBeach.desc: Expert AC services for Miami Beach condos, hotels, and oceanfront homes. Salt air demands specialized care.
miamiBeach.longDesc: Miami Beach's oceanfront location means your AC system faces unique challenges — salt air corrosion, high humidity, and near-constant operation. Whether you're in a South Beach high-rise, a Mid-Beach condo, or a North Beach family home, MGM A/C Appliances understands the specific HVAC needs of barrier island living. We provide corrosion-resistant solutions and regular maintenance plans designed for coastal environments.
miamiBeach.housingInfo: Miami Beach housing spans Art Deco gems from the 1930s-1940s — many converted to boutique hotels or multi-unit residences — and modern luxury towers along Collins Avenue and South Beach. Historic buildings frequently have PTAC units installed through exterior walls, while newer high-rises use central chilled-water systems. Ductwork is rare in pre-war buildings; most rely on ductless or window-unit solutions that are particularly vulnerable to coastal conditions.
miamiBeach.climateChallenge: Salt air corrosion is the single greatest AC threat in Miami Beach — the ocean breeze deposits salt crystals on condenser coils within months, eating through aluminum fins and copper lines at an accelerated rate compared to inland locations. Combined with periodic flooding from king tides and storm surge, exterior AC condensers on ground-level units face moisture intrusion that destroys electrical components. The relentless coastal humidity also means drain lines clog every 4-6 weeks rather than the standard 3-month interval.
commonIssues: Accelerated condenser coil corrosion from salt-laden ocean air — fins deteriorate 2-3x faster than inland
PTAC unit failures in Art Deco buildings due to constant marine environment exposure
Flood-damaged electrical components in ground-floor condenser units after king tides or storms
Drain pan overflow in condensate systems overwhelmed by extreme coastal humidity levels
miamiBeach.localTip: In Miami Beach, apply a condenser coil coating spray every season and rinse the outdoor unit with fresh water monthly — this simple habit can double the lifespan of your equipment by neutralizing salt buildup before it corrodes the fins.

testimonial.text: Living on Ocean Drive means my AC takes a beating from the salt air. MGM put a protective coating on my condenser and now I get yearly tune-ups instead of replacing units every two years. Worth every penny.
testimonial.name: Sofia R.

miamiGardens.name: Miami Gardens
miamiGardens.desc: Reliable AC repair and installation for Miami Gardens families. Same-day service, honest pricing.
miamiGardens.longDesc: Miami Gardens is one of the largest cities in Miami-Dade County, and its residents deserve fast, dependable AC service. MGM A/C Appliances provides same-day repair, professional installation, and preventive maintenance throughout Miami Gardens, Opa-locka, and surrounding communities. Our bilingual team is ready to serve you.
miamiGardens.housingInfo: Miami Gardens is primarily a suburban single-family neighborhood with homes built predominantly from the 1990s through the early 2000s, giving it a relatively modern housing stock compared to older Miami-Dade municipalities. Many homes feature 3-4 bedroom floor plans with 2,000-2,500 square feet requiring 3-5 ton systems. The area's CBS construction holds heat well, but many homeowners have deferred duct sealing and insulation upgrades since the original installation.
miamiGardens.climateChallenge: Miami Gardens absorbed significant hurricane damage from multiple storms, and many duct systems were temporarily repaired after storm damage rather than properly replaced — creating systems with leaks that silently waste 20-30% of conditioned air. The area's flat terrain and storm-prone location means outdoor units need proper elevation and hurricane strapping that many older installations lack. Summer heat loads here are intense given the lack of coastal breeze and the suburban density.
commonIssues: Hurricane-damaged ductwork with temporary repairs causing significant conditioned air loss
Improperly strapped outdoor condensers vulnerable to wind damage during storm season
Aging 1990s air handlers with deteriorating insulation lining and cracked drain pans
Thermostat communication failures in older systems incompatible with smart thermostat upgrades
miamiGardens.localTip: Before hurricane season, have your outdoor condenser professionally strapped and elevated if it sits on a ground pad — FEMA guidelines recommend at least 12 inches of elevation in this flood-prone zone, and proper strapping can prevent a $4,000 condenser replacement after a storm.

testimonial.text: After the last hurricane, our AC ductwork was a mess. MGM did a full duct inspection and found three spots where cool air was leaking into the attic. Fixed them all in one visit — our house finally gets cool evenly now.
testimonial.name: Darnell W.

aventura.name: Aventura
aventura.desc: Premium AC services for Aventura's luxury condos and residential communities. Licensed and insured.
aventura.longDesc: Aventura is home to some of South Florida's most prestigious residential towers and shopping destinations. MGM A/C Appliances serves Aventura's unique mix of high-rise condos, townhomes, and commercial spaces with expert HVAC solutions. From Aventura Mall-area businesses to waterfront condominiums, we deliver reliable comfort.
aventura.housingInfo: Aventura is defined by its luxury residential towers — buildings like Williams Island, Porto Vita, and the Turnberry complex — plus the Aventura Mall commercial campus. Most residential units are served by central chilled-water systems managed by building engineers, with individual fan coil units in each unit that residents are responsible for maintaining. High-end single-family homes in the area feature premium systems from brands like Trane, Lennox, and Carrier with advanced variable-speed technology.
aventura.climateChallenge: High-rise living creates unique HVAC challenges: fan coil units in individual condo units depend on the building's central chilled-water plant operating correctly, meaning residents can experience cooling problems caused by building-level issues that require coordination with HOA management. Salt air from the Intracoastal Waterway affects outdoor HVAC equipment on balconies and rooftops. Demanding clientele expectations mean service calls require same-day response and white-glove professionalism.
commonIssues: Fan coil unit valve failures requiring coordination with building management for chilled-water shutoff
Thermostat or controls failures in premium smart home integrated systems
Condensate drain blockages causing water damage in high-rise units — critical to address immediately
Salt corrosion on rooftop package units and balcony-mounted mini-split condensers
aventura.localTip: Aventura condo owners should know whether their building uses a chilled-water system or independent split systems — if it's chilled water, your responsibility is limited to the fan coil unit and thermostat inside your unit, while the building handles everything else. Knowing this before calling for service saves time and money.

testimonial.text: Our penthouse unit had been running warm for weeks. MGM diagnosed a failing fan coil valve and coordinated directly with our building engineer for the water shutoff. Professional, efficient, and they knew exactly how high-rise systems work.
testimonial.name: Jennifer L.

theKeys.name: Florida Keys
theKeys.desc: AC services for the Florida Keys — from Key Largo to Key West. We keep paradise cool.
theKeys.longDesc: The Florida Keys' tropical climate and salt air create some of the harshest conditions for HVAC systems anywhere in the country. With year-round heat, extreme humidity, and constant salt exposure, your AC needs specialized attention. MGM A/C Appliances serves the entire island chain, providing expert installation, repair, and maintenance with equipment and techniques built for island life.
theKeys.housingInfo: The Florida Keys present the most extreme HVAC environment in the continental United States — virtually every structure sits within a quarter mile of saltwater, and the island geography means there is no escape from the marine environment. Housing ranges from Old Florida fishing cottages elevated on pilings to modern concrete construction designed to survive Category 4 hurricanes. Many properties are vacation rentals with remote management, requiring reliable, low-maintenance systems that don't need constant attention when owners are absent.
theKeys.climateChallenge: Salt corrosion in the Keys is not just accelerated — it's aggressive beyond what mainland technicians typically encounter. Condenser coil fins can be completely corroded through within 18-24 months without protective treatment, a timeline that would take 5-7 years in an inland location. The limited contractor access in the Keys — particularly below Key Largo — means emergency service calls involve significant travel, making preventive maintenance even more critical. Humidity in the Keys stays at 70-80% year-round, with no dry season.
commonIssues: Extreme salt corrosion destroying condenser coils within 18-24 months without protective coatings
Remote property AC failures discovered only when guests arrive, requiring urgent emergency response
Humidity damage to electrical components and insulation from year-round 70-80% relative humidity
Wind damage to outdoor equipment during hurricane season with limited local service capacity
theKeys.localTip: Keys property owners — especially absentee vacation rental owners — should install a smart thermostat with remote monitoring and WiFi-connected alerts: if the system stops cooling, you'll know within hours rather than when a guest checks in. Paired with annual professional maintenance and coil coating, this is the best investment you can make in your Keys AC system.

testimonial.text: We own a vacation rental in Islamorada and finding reliable AC service was always a nightmare. MGM now does annual maintenance and installed a smart thermostat that alerts us if anything goes wrong. Our guests haven't had a single AC complaint since.
testimonial.name: Tom and Linda B.

fortLauderdale.name: Fort Lauderdale
fortLauderdale.desc: Full-service AC solutions for Fort Lauderdale homes and businesses. Broward County's trusted AC experts.
fortLauderdale.longDesc: Fort Lauderdale's mix of waterfront properties, urban condos, and suburban neighborhoods each present unique cooling challenges. MGM A/C Appliances brings our trusted South Florida service north to Broward County, providing the same honest pricing, bilingual support, and expert workmanship that's made us Miami-Dade's choice for AC service.
fortLauderdale.housingInfo: Fort Lauderdale's housing stock is incredibly varied — from 1950s-1960s concrete block homes in neighborhoods like Tarpon River and Riverside Park to luxury canal-front estates in Harbor Beach and Las Olas Isles, plus modern mid-rise condos in the Victoria Park and Flagler Village corridors. Canal-front properties face similar salt air challenges as Miami Beach due to tidal saltwater in the Intracoastal system. The city's booming vacation rental market means many properties are managed remotely, increasing the value of preventive maintenance programs.

maintenance-tips.title: 5 AC Maintenance Tips Every South Florida Homeowner Should Know
maintenance-tips.excerpt: Keep your AC running efficiently and avoid costly breakdowns with these essential maintenance tips for the South Florida climate.
content: Living in South Florida means your AC works harder than almost anywhere else in the country. With temperatures regularly hitting the 90s and humidity levels that can make it feel even hotter, your air conditioning system is your most important home appliance. Here are five essential maintenance tips to keep it running smoothly.

**1. Change Your Air Filter Every 30-60 Days** — In South Florida's dusty, humid environment, air filters clog faster than in other climates. A dirty filter forces your system to work harder, increasing energy bills and reducing its lifespan. Set a monthly reminder to check your filter.

**2. Keep Your Outdoor Unit Clear** — Your condenser unit needs at least 2 feet of clearance on all sides for proper airflow. Trim back any bushes, vines, or debris that might be blocking it. In South Florida, tropical plants grow fast — check this monthly.

**3. Schedule Professional Maintenance Twice a Year** — While some maintenance tasks you can do yourself, a professional tune-up twice a year is essential. A licensed technician will check refrigerant levels, clean coils, tighten electrical connections, and catch small problems before they become big ones.

**4. Monitor Your Thermostat Settings** — Setting your thermostat to 78°F when you're home and slightly higher when you're away can save you up to 10% on cooling costs. Consider a programmable thermostat if you don't already have one.

**5. Watch for Warning Signs** — Unusual noises, uneven cooling, higher-than-normal energy bills, or water around the unit are all signs that something may be wrong. Catching issues early saves money and prevents emergency breakdowns.

when-to-replace.title: When Should You Replace Your AC? 7 Signs It's Time
when-to-replace.excerpt: Not sure if your AC needs a repair or a full replacement? Here are the telltale signs that it's time for a new system.
content: Deciding whether to repair or replace your AC system is one of the biggest home maintenance decisions you'll face. Here are seven signs that it might be time for a new system.

**1. Your System Is Over 15 Years Old** — The average lifespan of an AC unit in South Florida is 10-15 years. If your system is approaching or past this age, it's likely becoming less efficient and more prone to breakdowns.
**2. Repair Costs Are Adding Up** — A good rule of thumb: if a repair costs more than 50% of the price of a new system, replacement is usually the smarter investment.

**3. Your Energy Bills Keep Rising** — If your energy bills are climbing despite no changes in usage, your aging AC system may be losing efficiency. Modern systems can be 30-50% more efficient than units from 10-15 years ago.

**4. Your System Uses R-22 Refrigerant** — R-22 (Freon) has been phased out due to environmental regulations. If your system uses it, replacement is inevitable — and the sooner you switch, the sooner you start saving.
**5. Uneven Cooling Throughout Your Home** — If some rooms are too cold while others are too warm, your system may no longer be capable of properly conditioning your space.
**6. Excessive Humidity Inside** — Your AC should moderate humidity as well as temperature. If your home feels muggy even with the AC running, the system may need replacement.

**7. Frequent Breakdowns** — If you're calling for repairs more than twice a year, it's a sign that your system is nearing the end of its useful life. A new system will give you reliability and peace of mind.

miami-climate-hvac.title: How Miami's Climate Affects Your HVAC System
miami-climate-hvac.excerpt: Understanding how South Florida's unique climate impacts your AC helps you make smarter maintenance and replacement decisions.
content: Miami's tropical climate is paradise for people but punishing for HVAC systems. Understanding how our unique weather conditions affect your AC helps you maintain it properly and make informed decisions about repairs and replacements.

**The Heat Factor** — Miami averages 248 days per year above 80°F. Your AC runs almost year-round, which means more wear and tear than systems in northern climates. This constant operation is why maintenance is so important here.

**Humidity: The Hidden Enemy** — South Florida's humidity levels regularly exceed 70%. Your AC doesn't just cool the air — it also dehumidifies it. This double duty puts extra strain on the system and can lead to issues like frozen coils and mold growth if not properly maintained.

**Salt Air Corrosion** — If you live near the coast, salt air can corrode your outdoor AC unit's metal components. Condenser coils, fan blades, and housing all suffer from saltwater exposure. Regular cleaning and anti-corrosion treatments can extend your unit's life.

**Hurricane Season Impact** — Miami's hurricane season (June through November) brings debris, power surges, and flooding — all of which can damage HVAC equipment. After any major storm, have your system inspected before turning it back on.

**Mold and Indoor Air Quality** — The combination of heat and humidity makes South Florida a hotspot for mold growth. Your AC system plays a critical role in controlling indoor humidity and maintaining air quality. A well-maintained system keeps mold at bay; a neglected one can actually spread it.

**What You Can Do** — The best defense against Miami's climate is proactive maintenance. Schedule professional tune-ups twice a year, change filters monthly, keep the outdoor unit clear, and address any issues immediately. A well-maintained system in South Florida can last 12-15 years; a neglected one may fail in 7-8.

what-is-the-5000-rule.title: What Is the $5,000 AC Rule? A Miami Homeowner's Guide
what-is-the-5000-rule.excerpt: The $5,000 rule is the fastest way to decide whether to repair or replace your AC — and in Miami's heat, it matters more than ever.
content: **The $5,000 rule is simple: multiply your AC unit's age by the cost of the repair. If that number exceeds $5,000, it's time to replace the unit rather than repair it.** This rule of thumb has been used by HVAC professionals for decades, and it's especially useful for Miami homeowners where AC systems work harder — and wear out faster — than almost anywhere else in the country.

Here's how the math works with real Miami examples. Suppose you have a **10-year-old unit** and the technician quotes you **$600 to replace the compressor capacitor and clean the coils**. Multiply 10 × $600 = $6,000. That exceeds $5,000, which means replacement is likely the smarter financial move. On the other hand, if you have a **5-year-old unit** and need a **$400 contactor replacement**, the math is 5 × $400 = $2,000 — well under $5,000, so repairing makes sense.

Why does Miami change the calculus? In most of the United States, a central AC system lasts **15–20 years**. Here in South Florida, the realistic lifespan is **10–15 years** — sometimes less. Your system runs 8 to 10 months out of the year, battling temperatures in the 90s, humidity above 70%, and salt air if you're anywhere near the coast in neighborhoods like Miami Beach, Key Biscayne, or Coconut Grove. That constant operation adds up fast.

**The most expensive AC repairs** are the ones most likely to trigger the $5,000 rule. Compressor replacement in Miami typically runs **$1,500–$2,500** depending on unit size and brand. Evaporator coil replacement costs **$1,000–$2,000**. A refrigerant leak repair that also requires recharging the system can run **$600–$1,200**. On a 12-year-old unit, any of these repairs will push you well over the $5,000 threshold.

There's a secondary consideration many homeowners overlook: **refrigerant type**. If your system was installed before 2010, it likely uses R-22 refrigerant (Freon), which has been phased out under EPA regulations. R-22 now costs $100–$150 per pound on the open market — and a typical recharge takes 2–5 pounds. An aging R-22 system failing in Doral or Kendall isn't just expensive to fix; every repair is a temporary patch on a system you'll be forced to replace eventually anyway.

The $5,000 rule also helps you avoid the **"repair trap"** — the cycle of fixing one thing only to have something else break two months later. Once a system crosses the 12-year mark in South Florida, components start failing in clusters. The capacitor goes, then the contactor, then the fan motor. Each repair individually might seem reasonable, but they add up quickly, and you're still running an inefficient old unit that's spiking your FPL bill every month.

**Energy efficiency is a hidden part of the equation.** An older system might be rated at 10–13 SEER (Seasonal Energy Efficiency Ratio), while a new system is 16–20+ SEER. In Miami, where your AC runs almost year-round, upgrading to a higher-SEER unit can reduce your cooling costs by **20–40%**. With FPL rates averaging around $0.13–$0.15 per kWh, those savings are real. On a typical 2,000 sq ft Miami home, a new efficient system can save $400–$800 per year in electricity alone.

That said, the $5,000 rule isn't absolute. **Context matters.** If your unit is 9 years old but has been exceptionally well-maintained with twice-yearly service, and the repair needed is a simple one-time fix, it may be worth repairing even if the math is close. Conversely, if your unit is 8 years old but has had three major repairs in two years, replacement might be wise even if the formula says repair.

**When to always replace regardless of the rule:** if the system still uses R-22 Freon; if it's been flooding your home due to persistent drain line issues that keep returning; if it's causing mold problems from insufficient dehumidification; or if your FPL bills have climbed 25% or more over the past two summers without explanation.

The bottom line for Miami homeowners: the $5,000 rule is your starting point, not your final answer. Use it to frame the conversation with your technician, factor in your unit's refrigerant type and maintenance history, and weigh the energy savings of a new high-efficiency system. In South Florida's demanding climate, the decision to replace a borderline unit often pays for itself faster than you'd expect.

**Ready to find out if your AC is worth repairing?** MGM A/C Appliances offers free estimates for South Florida homeowners. Our technicians will give you an honest assessment — no upselling, just the facts. Call us at **(786) 352-0084** and we'll help you make the smartest decision for your home and your budget.

ac-repair-cost-miami.title: How Much Does AC Repair Cost in Miami? (2026 Price Guide)
ac-repair-cost-miami.excerpt: AC repair costs in Miami range from $150 for a capacitor swap to $2,500 for a compressor — here's what you can expect to pay in 2026.
content: **AC repair costs in Miami in 2026 typically range from $150 to $2,500 depending on the part and the complexity of the job.** Miami homeowners pay roughly 10–20% more than the national average for HVAC repairs — a reflection of year-round demand, the local cost of living, and the fact that South Florida systems work harder than units almost anywhere else in the country. Here's a complete breakdown of what you can expect to pay.

**Diagnostic Fee: $75–$150.** Most reputable HVAC companies in Miami charge a diagnostic or service call fee to come out, evaluate the system, and identify the problem. At MGM A/C Appliances, our diagnostic fee is applied toward the cost of the repair if you proceed with us. Be wary of companies that offer "free diagnostics" and then dramatically inflate the repair estimate.

**Capacitor Replacement: $150–$400.** The capacitor is the most commonly replaced AC component in South Florida. It's a small cylindrical part that helps start and run the compressor and fan motors. Miami's heat causes capacitors to fail far more frequently than in cooler climates — many South Florida homeowners replace one every 3–5 years. The part itself is inexpensive ($15–$50), so the bulk of the cost is labor.

**Contactor Replacement: $150–$350.** The contactor is an electrical relay switch that controls power to the compressor and condenser fan. Like capacitors, contactors wear out faster in hot climates and are a routine repair in Miami. This is typically a 30–45 minute job for an experienced technician.

**Refrigerant Recharge: $200–$600.** If your system is low on refrigerant, it won't cool properly. A recharge involves checking for leaks, repairing them if found, and adding the correct refrigerant. The cost varies significantly based on the **type of refrigerant** your system uses. Systems using the modern **R-410A** refrigerant are less expensive to service. Systems still running on the phased-out **R-22 Freon** can cost $400–$800 or more just for the refrigerant, since R-22 now runs $100–$150 per pound.

**Fan Motor Replacement: $300–$700.** There are two fan motors in a typical split AC system — the blower motor (inside, moves air over the evaporator coil) and the condenser fan motor (outside, draws air over the condenser coil). Salt air in coastal Miami neighborhoods like Surfside, Bay Harbor Islands, and Miami Beach is particularly hard on outdoor fan motors, corroding the bearings and windings over time.

**Compressor Replacement: $1,500–$2,500.** The compressor is the heart of your AC system — and the most expensive component to replace. If the compressor fails on a unit that's 10+ years old, it almost always makes more sense to replace the entire system rather than just the compressor (see our post on the $5,000 rule). For newer units under warranty, compressor replacement is worth pursuing.

**Evaporator Coil Replacement: $1,000–$2,000.** The evaporator coil sits inside your air handler and is where the actual heat exchange happens. In Miami's humid environment, evaporator coils are prone to developing mold and to corrosion — particularly in homes near the coast. A leaking or corroded evaporator coil is a significant repair that, on an older unit, often tips the scale toward full replacement.

**Circuit Board (Control Board) Replacement: $400–$800.** Modern AC systems are controlled by circuit boards that manage every aspect of operation. Lightning strikes — common in South Florida during the June–November storm season — are a leading cause of circuit board failures in Miami-area homes. A surge protector on your AC disconnect is a worthwhile $50–$100 investment to protect against this.

**What drives Miami AC repair prices higher than the national average?** First, year-round demand means technicians are busy and labor rates reflect that. Second, the sheer number of AC systems per capita — virtually every home and business runs AC 365 days a year — creates sustained demand. Third, the corrosive effects of humidity and salt air mean parts wear out faster, increasing the frequency of repairs.

**Emergency and after-hours service** can add $100–$200 to any repair bill at most companies. At MGM A/C Appliances, we don't charge extra for after-hours or weekend service calls — our price is our price, day or night. We also provide **free estimates** so you know the full cost before we start any work.
